executive_brief: The cyber threat landscape has fundamentally shifted from opportunistic crime to industrialized, AI-accelerated warfare. Over the past week, threat actors compressed the traditional vulnerability exploitation window from months to hours, forcing CERT-In to mandate a strict 12-hour patching SLA for internet-facing assets [41, 107, 189]. This velocity is not theoretical; AI models are autonomously chaining exploits, generating phishing campaigns, and weaponizing zero-days before defenders can triage reports. Simultaneously, the software supply chain is collapsing under coordinated assaults targeting developer tooling, CI/CD pipelines, and package registries, with campaigns like Megalodon and Laravel-Lang poisoning infecting thousands of repositories and build environments [8, 30, 53, 60, 176]. Defenders who rely on static patching cycles, traditional MFA, or perimeter-based security are already losing ground.

threat_stories.headline: The 12-Hour Clock: AI Compresses the Exploitation Window to Survival Mode
threat_stories.narrative: The era of 30-day patching windows ended this week. CERT-In issued a directive mandating that critical internet-facing vulnerabilities be patched, mitigated, or isolated within 12 hours [41, 107, 189]. This mandate responds to a documented reality: threat actors now deploy AI-driven reconnaissance, automated exploit generation, and machine-speed deployment pipelines that bypass human-led defense cycles entirely [37, 162]. The velocity is unprecedented. Anthropic’s Project Glasswing alone identified 10,000 high and critical vulnerabilities across 1,000 open-source projects in a single month, exposing the massive backlog of unpatched infrastructure [16, 42, 74]. Defenders cannot outpace AI with manual triage. The new operational baseline requires automated vulnerability prioritization tied to real-time exploitation telemetry, dynamic network segmentation, and pre-approved emergency mitigation playbooks. Organizations treating patching as a monthly chore face immediate compromise.

timeline.event: CERT-In publishes advisory mandating 12-hour patching for internet-facing crown jewels amid AI-assisted exploitation acceleration
timeline.significance: Establishes a new regulatory baseline for vulnerability response, acknowledging that traditional SLAs are obsolete against AI-driven attack chains

timeline.event: Anthropic Project Glasswing disclosure reveals 10,000+ vulnerabilities across open-source ecosystems with a 90.6% true-positive rate
timeline.significance: Demonstrates the scale of undiscovered flaws and highlights the human remediation bottleneck that leaves infrastructure exposed

timeline.event: Industry analysis confirms adversarial AI has industrialized exploitation, chaining misconfigurations and stale credentials at machine speed
timeline.significance: Validates the CERT-In mandate and forces enterprises to adopt Continuous Threat Exposure Management (CTEM) over CVSS-driven patching

threat_stories.impact_assessment: All organizations with internet-facing assets, cloud infrastructure, and open-source dependencies. Severity is critical due to the collapse of traditional defense timelines and the automated nature of exploitation campaigns.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement automated vulnerability scanning tied to CISA KEV and real-time threat intel feeds. Deploy emergency mitigation playbooks (WAF rules, network isolation, access restriction) executable within 12 hours. Replace CVSS-driven patching with exploitability-based prioritization. Enforce microsegmentation to contain lateral movement if initial access occurs.

threat_stories.headline: Supply Chain Carnage: Developer Tooling Becomes the Primary Attack Surface
threat_stories.narrative: Attackers have abandoned the perimeter and moved directly into the build pipeline. This week, the Megalodon campaign abused compromised GitHub Personal Access Tokens to inject base64-encoded bash payloads into GitHub Actions workflows across 5,500 repositories, exfiltrating cloud credentials and OIDC tokens to a centralized C2 server [8, 53]. Simultaneously, threat actors executed a sophisticated Git tag poisoning attack against Laravel-Lang Composer packages, rewriting over 700 historical tags to point to malicious forks that deploy a cross-platform PHP information stealer [30, 60]. The TanStack and Nx Console compromises further demonstrate how CI/CD pipelines and IDE extensions are being weaponized to harvest developer secrets [176]. The GlassWorm botnet takedown proves these campaigns are highly organized and resilient, but the underlying vulnerability remains: developer environments lack the security controls applied to production workloads [84, 117, 131, 190]. Supply chain attacks are no longer opportunistic; they are the primary delivery mechanism for enterprise compromise.

timeline.event: Megalodon campaign begins injecting base64-encoded workflow_dispatch payloads into GitHub Actions across 5,500 public repositories
timeline.significance: Bypasses pull request reviews and directly compromises CI/CD pipelines to harvest cloud credentials and OIDC tokens

timeline.event: Laravel-Lang Git tag poisoning attack rewrites 700+ historical tags, deploying PHP information stealer via Composer autoloader
timeline.significance: Exploits trust in version control tagging, infecting developer workstations and build runners without modifying source code

timeline.event: CISA adds TanStack, Nx Console, and DAEMON Tools supply chain compromises to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
timeline.significance: Officially recognizes IDE extensions and npm packages as critical attack vectors requiring immediate enterprise response

timeline.event: CrowdStrike and Google dismantle GlassWorm botnet infrastructure, but analysts warn repository poisoning remains systemic
timeline.significance: Confirms the scale of open-source supply chain threats and highlights the need for continuous dependency verification

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Software development teams, DevOps pipelines, cloud infrastructure, and any organization relying on npm, Composer, or GitHub Actions. Severity is critical due to direct credential theft and persistent CI/CD compromise.
threat_stories.action_required: Audit all GitHub Actions workflows for unauthorized base64-encoded scripts and workflow_dispatch triggers. Rotate all PATs, deploy keys, and CI/CD secrets immediately. Implement strict branch protection rules requiring PR reviews for workflow changes. Deploy dependency scanning tools during development, not just in CI. Enforce IDE extension allowlists and verify package integrity hashes before installation.

threat_stories.headline: The MFA Death Spiral: PhaaS, Kali365, and OAuth Token Theft
threat_stories.narrative: Multifactor authentication is functionally broken for organizations relying on SMS, OTP, or standard push notifications. The Kali365 phishing service now bypasses Microsoft 365 MFA entirely by tricking users into entering legitimate OAuth device codes on spoofed landing pages, granting attackers valid access and refresh tokens that never trigger MFA challenges [21, 38, 73, 79]. Parallel campaigns leverage Chinese-language Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platforms to intercept OTPs in real-time. When victims enter credentials on AI-generated phishing pages, attackers simultaneously trigger OTP requests on their own devices, capturing the code seconds before expiration and provisioning stolen payment cards into digital wallets [6, 187]. Signal users face targeted SMS campaigns stealing 64-character backup recovery keys, granting full decryption of historical message archives [221, 250]. These campaigns prove that MFA is only as strong as the weakest delivery channel. Without phishing-resistant authentication, credential theft is inevitable.

timeline.event: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing kit exploiting Microsoft OAuth device code flow to bypass MFA and harvest access tokens
timeline.significance: Demonstrates how legitimate authentication protocols are abused to maintain persistent access without triggering security alerts

timeline.event: GTIG analysis reveals Chinese PhaaS platforms using AI-generated pages and real-time OTP interception to provision digital wallets
timeline.significance: Confirms that SMS/OTP-based MFA is systematically bypassed through live administrative panels and encrypted messaging delivery

timeline.event: Targeted phishing campaigns exploit Signal Secure Backups by stealing 64-character recovery keys via SMS impersonation
timeline.significance: Shows threat actors targeting high-value individuals to bypass end-to-end encryption and access historical communications

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Enterprises using Microsoft 365, SMS/OTP-based MFA, and cloud storage providers. Severity is critical due to persistent access, token theft, and complete MFA bypass.
threat_stories.action_required: Enforce FIDO2/WebAuthn security keys for all privileged and remote access accounts. Disable or strictly restrict Microsoft OAuth device code authentication flows via Conditional Access policies. Implement continuous access evaluation and monitor for unauthorized device registrations and malicious inbox rules. Replace SMS-based verification with out-of-band or hardware-backed authentication.

threat_stories.headline: Enterprise Software Under Siege: FortiClient, Ghost, and GlobalProtect Exploited at Scale
threat_stories.narrative: Widely deployed enterprise software is being actively exploited with zero-day precision. FortiClient EMS (CVE-2026-35616) suffers from an authentication bypass that allows attackers to push the EKZ Infostealer disguised as legitimate Fortinet patches, compromising endpoint management infrastructure at scale [147, 184, 193, 266]. Ghost CMS instances face mass compromise via CVE-2026-26980, an unauthenticated SQL injection in the Content API that enables database exfiltration and ClickFix malware injection across 700+ sites, including Harvard and Oxford [12, 28, 54]. Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect (CVE-2026-0257) allows authentication bypass through forged VPN cookies when certificate configurations overlap, granting unauthorized network access [251, 258, 268]. These vulnerabilities share a common trait: they exploit trusted systems and default configurations. Defenders cannot rely on vendor trust or perimeter isolation. Immediate patching and compensating controls are mandatory.

timeline.event: Threat actors exploit CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS to deploy EKZ Infostealer via fake vendor patches
timeline.significance: Compromises trusted endpoint management infrastructure, enabling credential theft across managed device fleets

timeline.event: CVE-2026-0257 authentication bypass in Palo Alto GlobalProtect allows forged VPN cookie exploitation
timeline.significance: Grants unauthorized remote access to corporate networks when certificate configurations are misaligned

timeline.event: Ghost CMS mass exploitation via CVE-2026-26980 SQL injection fuels ClickFix ad-fraud campaigns across academic and tech sites
timeline.significance: Demonstrates rapid weaponization of unauthenticated web application flaws for financial malware distribution

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Organizations running FortiClient EMS, Palo Alto GlobalProtect, Ghost CMS, or default enterprise configurations. Severity is critical due to active exploitation, credential theft, and persistent remote access.
threat_stories.action_required: Patch FortiClient EMS to version 7.4.7 immediately and verify patch signatures before deployment. Upgrade Palo Alto PAN-OS and enforce dedicated certificates for cookie encryption separate from HTTPS services. Update Ghost CMS and rotate all Admin API keys. Deploy WAF rules to block known exploit payloads and restrict management interface access to trusted IP ranges.

actor_profiles.name: GREYVIBE
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Leveraging AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, Ideogram) to generate custom malware (LegionRelay), craft phishing lures, and manage infrastructure. Deploying five distinct attack chains (PhantomMail, PhantomClick, PrincessClub, DroneLink, Nebo) targeting Ukrainian military, government, and civilian sectors. Exhibiting OpSec failures including test sample uploads and informal development artifacts.

actor_profiles.name: ShinyHunters
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executing large-scale data breaches against Carnival Corporation (6 million records) and Charter Communications (4.9 million accounts) via social engineering and credential compromise. Exfiltrating personal data, membership statuses, and internal identifiers. Threatening public leaks to force ransom payments.

actor_profiles.name: Silent Ransom Group
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deploying operatives to physically visit target locations, impersonating IT support to convince employees to insert malicious USB drives or grant remote desktop access. Installing RATs, keyloggers, and data exfiltration tools (WinSCP, Rclone) to steal sensitive data for extortion. Targeting law firms and corporate offices.

actor_profiles.name: Kali365 Operators
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial gain, credential theft, MFA bypass
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Operating a sophisticated Phishing-as-a-Service platform targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. Exploiting OAuth device code flow to bypass MFA, harvest access and refresh tokens, and maintain persistent access without triggering security alerts. Using live administrative panels and AI-generated phishing pages.

actor_profiles.name: Megalodon / TanStack Attackers
actor_profiles.motivation: Credential theft, CI/CD pipeline compromise, financial gain
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Abusing compromised GitHub PATs and deploy keys to inject base64-encoded bash payloads into GitHub Actions workflows across 5,500 repositories. Compromising 42 @tanstack npm packages via cache poisoning, pull_request_target misconfiguration, and OIDC token theft. Distributing malicious Nx Console and DAEMON Tools installers.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unauthenticated SQL injection in the Content API allows attackers to read arbitrary database contents, steal Admin API keys, and inject malicious JavaScript payloads into published pages.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Ghost CMS to the latest patched version immediately. Rotate all Admin API keys and database credentials. Deploy WAF rules to block SQL injection payloads targeting Content API endpoints.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded ASP.NET machineKey values across deployments enable ViewState deserialization attacks, granting unauthenticated remote code execution and web shell deployment.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Generate and deploy unique, cryptographically strong machine keys per instance. Update web.config files to remove hardcoded keys. Apply vendor patches and implement WAF rules blocking deserialization payloads.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper access control allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication via API endpoints, enabling privilege escalation and remote code execution to deploy credential-stealing malware.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade FortiClient EMS to version 7.4.7 immediately. Verify patch signatures before deployment. Restrict EMS console access to trusted administrative IP ranges.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication bypass vulnerability allows attackers to forge VPN authentication cookies when the same certificate is used for HTTPS and cookie encryption, granting unauthorized network access.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade PAN-OS to the patched version. Generate and use a dedicated certificate exclusively for cookie encryption, ensuring it is not shared with the HTTPS service.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unsafe deserialization of untrusted data allows authenticated attackers with minimum Site Member permissions to execute arbitrary code remotely.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest Microsoft security updates for all supported SharePoint Server versions immediately. Validate patch deployment across on-premises and cloud instances.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper handling of Redis enable/disable functions allows any cPanel user to execute arbitrary scripts with root privileges.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin to version 2.4.7 or later. Review cPanel logs for suspicious Redis-related API calls and block unauthorized IPs.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unpatched Windows zero-day vulnerability (YellowKey) affecting Defender and BitLocker components, publicly disclosed without vendor coordination. Working POC code published.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Microsoft security updates immediately upon release. Implement strict network segmentation and application whitelisting to limit exploit propagation. Monitor MSRC advisories.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unauthenticated remote attackers can pull private container images from affected deployments without credentials, exposing proprietary software and internal configurations.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade Gitea to version 1.26.2 or later. Restrict network access to Gitea container registries and rotate any exposed credentials.

hunting_leads.title: Ghost CMS ClickFix Injection & SQLi Activity
hunting_leads.context: Attackers exploit CVE-2026-26980 to inject malicious JavaScript and harvest Admin API keys. Hunting for anomalous Content API requests and injected scripts is critical.
hunting_leads.query: index=web_logs "GET /ghost/api/v3/content" OR "POST /ghost/api/v3/content" | regex request_uri=".*[\?&]filter=.*[\'\"\-].*" | stats count by src_ip, request_uri | where count > 5
indicators: Unauthenticated requests to /ghost/api/v3/content
SQL injection payloads in filter parameters
Injected JavaScript containing ClickFix domains
Unexpected Admin API key usage in logs

hunting_leads.title: FortiClient EMS EKZ Infostealer Deployment
hunting_leads.context: Threat actors abuse CVE-2026-35616 to push malicious payloads disguised as legitimate Fortinet patches. Detecting unauthorized EMS configuration pushes is essential.
hunting_leads.query: index=fortinet_ems ("EMS Update" OR "Endpoint Deployment") AND NOT (signature="verified" OR hash="known_good") | stats count by client_ip, deployed_payload, timestamp | where count > 0
indicators: EMS update pushes with mismatched digital signatures
EKZ Infostealer process execution (ekz.exe, ekz_service.exe)
Outbound HTTP traffic to unknown C2 domains from managed endpoints
Anomalous PowerShell commands disguised as vendor updates

hunting_leads.title: Kali365 OAuth Device Code Abuse
hunting_leads.context: Attackers trick users into entering OAuth device codes on spoofed pages, bypassing MFA. Monitoring for device code authentication flows and token anomalies is required.
hunting_leads.query: index=azure_ad signinlogs | where AuthenticationMethod =~ "Device Code" | where ResultType != 0 | stats count by UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, ResultType | where count > 2
indicators: Multiple device code authentication requests from single IPs
OAuth access/refresh token generation without MFA prompts
New device registrations in Microsoft 365 tenant
Malicious inbox rules created to suppress security alerts

hunting_leads.title: Megalodon GitHub Actions Workflow Injection
hunting_leads.context: Compromised PATs inject base64-encoded bash payloads into CI/CD workflows. Scanning for unauthorized workflow modifications and encoded scripts prevents credential theft.
hunting_leads.query: index=github_audit "workflow_dispatch" OR "pull_request_target" | regex action="base64.*decode" OR "curl.*http.*8443" | stats count by repo_name, actor, workflow_file | where count > 0
indicators: Unexpected workflow_dispatch executions
Base64-encoded bash payloads in .github/workflows/*.yml
Outbound connections to 216.126.225.129:8443
Forged commit authors (build-bot, auto-ci, ci-bot)

hunting_leads.title: Silent Ransom Group USB & Remote Access Tool Installation
hunting_leads.context: Physical operatives install RATs and data exfiltration tools via USB or social engineering. Detecting unauthorized remote access tools and bulk data transfers is critical.
hunting_leads.query: index=endpoint_processes (process_name="AnyDesk.exe" OR "TeamViewer.exe" OR "ZohoAssist.exe" OR "WinSCP.exe" OR "rclone.exe") AND NOT (user="admin" OR approved=true) | stats count by host, process_name, user | where count > 0
indicators: Installation of remote access tools (AnyDesk, RustDesk, Quick Assist)
USB device connections followed by process execution
Large outbound data transfers via WinSCP or Rclone
Unscheduled PowerShell scripts executing data compression/archiving

{'summary': 'Real-time MFA/OTP interception and credential harvesting via PhaaS platforms', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['SMS/OTP-based MFA systems', 'Digital Wallet Provisioning Services', 'RCS/iMessage Clients', 'Web Browsers'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': 0.0, 'workarounds': ['Enforce FIDO2/WebAuthn security keys for critical accounts', 'Delay or limit SMS/OTP delivery frequency', 'Implement step-up verification for digital wallet provisioning', 'Use email or voice calls for out-of-band verification instead of SMS/RCS'], 'references': ['Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) Analysis: 2 PhaaS 2 Furious', 'Article: 2 PhaaS 2 Furious: The Evolution of Chinese-language Phishing Services (2026-05-25)'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': "Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platforms enable attackers to bypass multifactor authentication (MFA) through real-time interception. When a victim enters credentials on a dynamically generated phishing page, the data is instantly relayed to an attacker's live administration panel. The attacker simultaneously triggers an OTP request on their own device, capturing the code seconds before expiration. Stolen credentials and OTPs are then used to provision payment cards into digital wallets on attacker-controlled devices, enabling tokenization for unauthorized transactions. Delivery leverages end-to-end encrypted channels like RCS and iMessage to bypass carrier security filters. AI-powered tools (e.g., Puppeteer) generate unique, high-fidelity phishing pages to evade signature-based detection, while anti-bot verification screens hinder automated security analysis.", 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'high', 'availability_impact': 'low', 'remediation': 'Transition authentication infrastructure to phishing-resistant standards such as FIDO2/WebAuthn. Implement risk-based authentication and device fingerprinting during digital wallet provisioning. Deploy on-device security controls and link inspection for RCS/iMessage. Utilize AI-driven behavioral analytics to detect anomalous login patterns and rapid OTP usage.', 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': 'N/A', 'cvss_vector': 'N/A', 'user_interaction': 'required', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '2026-05-25', 'discovery_date': ''}

statistics.key_changes: AI has compressed vulnerability exploitation timelines from months to hours, triggering mandatory 12-hour patching SLAs. Supply chain attacks now target developer tooling, CI/CD pipelines, and package registries at unprecedented scale. Traditional MFA is systematically bypassed via OAuth abuse and real-time OTP interception.
emerging_trends: AI-powered autonomous exploitation and malware generation
Physical-digital hybrid extortion (USB drops, in-person social engineering)
Uncoordinated zero-day dumps bypassing vendor coordination
AI agents weaponized for post-exploitation and credential harvesting
declining_threats: Standalone credential stuffing without MFA bypass
Basic phishing campaigns lacking real-time interception
Opportunistic ransomware without double extortion tactics
